figures.caption: Figure 1: Overview of TokenDecouple. We decouple token compression for TS and prompt tokens by merging redundant TS tokens in the frequency-domain and reducing prompt tokens under the pyramidal decay effect.
Figure 2: The complex TS appears entangled in the time domain but shows concentrated energy in a few frequency bands, while white noise has a nearly uniform spectrum, indicating poor compressibility.

Table 2: Performance comparison between the original models (Ori) and TokenDecouple (TD) on reasoningcentric tasks, evaluated using accuracy(%). Across 24 comparative experiments, TokenDecouple achieves better performance in 17 cases, accounting for 71%.
Figure 7: Statistical characteristics of datasets, including seasonality, trend, stationarity, transition, shifting, correlation, and non-Gaussianity.

Table 10: MAE results on the imputation task under 3 missing ratios(12.5%, 25%, and 50%), comparing the original models (Ori) and TokenDecouple (TD) on pattern-centric tasks. The results are obtained from 5 random seeds.

Table 11: MSE results on the imputation task under 3 missing ratios (12.5%, 25%, and 50%), comparing the original models (Ori) and TokenDecouple (TD) on pattern-centric tasks. The results are obtained from 5 random seeds.
Table 12: Accuracy results (%) on the classification task, comparing the original models (Ori) and TokenDecouple (TD) on pattern-centric tasks. The results are obtained from 5 random seeds.
Table 13: F1-score results on the anomaly detection task, comparing the original models (Ori) and TokenDecouple (TD) on pattern-centric tasks. The results are obtained from 5 random seeds.

Table 14: MAE results on the forecasting task under two prediction horizons (H = 192, 720), comparing the original models (Ori) and TokenDecouple (TD) on pattern-centric tasks. The results are obtained from 5 random seeds.

Table 15: MSE results on the forecasting task under two prediction horizons (H = 192, 720), comparing the original models (Ori) and TokenDecouple (TD) on pattern-centric tasks. The results are obtained from 5 random seeds.
Table 16: Algorithmic of TokenDecouple. The method decouples TS tokens and prompt tokens via frequency-aware TS token merging and pyramidal prompt token compression.
Figure 10: Prompt template for the Imputation task.
Figure 11: Prompt template for the Classification task.
Figure 12: Prompt template for the Anomaly Detection task.
Figure 13: Prompt template for the Forecasting task.
Figure 14: Prompt template for the Reasoning task.
